message:
fix(mcp): apply ReadOutput policy gate to wa.wait_for before waiting (resolves ft-dlyhk)

Closes the HIGH-severity bead filed earlier in this session
(`ft-dlyhk — [HIGH] wa.wait_for bypasses ReadOutput policy gate
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:1618-1684)`).

**Background.**

Of the MCP tools that read pane output — `wa.get_text`,
`wa.wait_for`, and the output side of `wa.search` — only
`wa.get_text` went through the `ReadOutput` capability check on the
configured policy engine before returning data. `wa.wait_for`
(implemented as a unit struct `WaWaitForTool;` with no state and no
config access) jumped straight into the wezterm wait loop. An
operator who had a policy rule denying `read_output` on, say, a
production-tagged pane domain could still observe the pane's output
by calling `wa.wait_for` with any pattern and letting it match on
freshly written text. This is a policy-bypass bug, not just a
missing feature — it defeats the gate for one of the three read
surfaces.

**Restructure** (crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs):

`WaWaitForTool` becomes a stateful struct carrying the same
dependencies the other policy-aware MCP tools carry:

    pub(super) struct WaWaitForTool {
        config: Arc<Config>,
        db_path: Option<Arc<PathBuf>>,
        wezterm: crate::wezterm::WeztermHandle,
        policy_rate_limiter: SharedRateLimiter,
    }

Four constructors, in the shape now standard across the MCP tool
module:

- `new(config, db_path)` — builds a fresh shared rate limiter from
  the config, uses the default wezterm handle.
- `new_with_shared_rate_limiter(config, db_path, policy_rate_limiter)`
  — accepts an externally owned rate limiter so the bridge can
  share one across multiple tools.
- `with_wezterm_handle(config, db_path, wezterm)` (cfg(test) only)
  — swaps in a MockWezterm handle for unit tests.
- `with_wezterm_handle_and_shared_rate_limiter(...)` — the full
  form the other three delegate into.

The impl of `ToolHandler::call` is rewritten around the
get_text-equivalent policy flow:

1. Open the storage handle from `db_path`.
2. Load any distributed remote pane metadata from storage.
3. Resolve the pane's domain (prefers live wezterm info, falls back
   to persisted remote-pane state).
4. Compute `capabilities` via `resolve_pane_capabilities`.
5. Build a `PolicyEngine` against the shared rate limiter.
6. Build `mcp_get_text_policy_input(...)` for this pane + domain +
   capabilities, enriched with the pane's title/cwd when available
   (from live pane info, else from the persisted remote-pane
   record).
7. Run `engine.authorize(&input)`. On deny: persist an audit
   record via `persist_mcp_policy_denial_async` with
   `DECISION_DENIED` / `REASON_CODE_DENIED` and return
   `McpToolError::new(MCP_ERR_POLICY, reason, POLICY_DENY_HINT)`.
   On `requires_approval`: attach to the decision via
   `ApprovalStore::attach_to_decision`, persist the audit record
   with `DECISION_REQUIRE_APPROVAL`, and return
   `MCP_ERR_POLICY` with the approval command hint.
8. Only if the decision resolves to allowed does the wait loop
   proceed. Unreachable-pane recovery preserved (distributed
   remote panes without a live wezterm pane still surface
   `PaneNotFound` at the right point).

**Bridge wiring** (`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_bridge.rs`):

`build_server_with_db` now constructs `WaWaitForTool` via
`new_with_shared_rate_limiter(config, db_path, shared_rate_limiter)`
instead of the old unit value, so the tool shares the rate limiter
with the other policy-aware tools on the server.

**Test additions:**

- `deny_mcp_read_output_config(domain, message)` helper in the test
  module builds a minimal `Config` with a single
  `PolicyRule { actors: ["mcp"], actions: ["read_output"],
  pane_domains: [<domain>], decision: Deny, .. }`. The builder
  makes the intent explicit in test code rather than scattering
  hand-built configs everywhere.
- `wait_for_tool_applies_policy_rules_before_waiting` — uses
  `MockWezterm` and a policy that denies read_output for the pane's
  domain. Invokes `wa.wait_for`, asserts the error is
  `McpToolError::new(MCP_ERR_POLICY, ...)` with the policy deny
  hint, and asserts the audit record was persisted.
- Updates the `mcp_manifest_matches_golden` golden surface (the
  fixed-list site) to the new `WaWaitForTool::new(Arc::clone(&cfg),
  Some(Arc::clone(&db)))` call shape so manifest generation keeps
  working under the new constructor.

No external wire-protocol change — tool name and arguments are
identical. The only observable difference for a client is that
calls that would previously have succeeded against a domain with
`deny read_output` now return `MCP_ERR_POLICY`, which is the
correct behavior.

Also captures one new audit bead insertion in
`.beads/issues.jsonl` (1 line added, 0 removed) — pure forward
progress in the issue tracker.

files.patch:
@@ -47,7 +47,13 @@ pub fn build_server_with_db(config: &Config, db_path: Option<PathBuf>) -> Result
             filter,
             db_path.clone(),
         )))
-        .tool(FormatAwareToolHandler::new(WaWaitForTool))
+        .tool(FormatAwareToolHandler::new(
+            WaWaitForTool::new_with_shared_rate_limiter(
+                Arc::clone(&config),
+                db_path.clone(),
+                Arc::clone(&shared_rate_limiter),
+            ),
+        ))
         .tool(FormatAwareToolHandler::new(WaRulesListTool))
         .tool(FormatAwareToolHandler::new(WaRulesTestTool))
         .tool(FormatAwareToolHandler::new(WaCassSearchTool))
